reasoning: Candidate (diving-helmet breakable target) fails on multiple fronts vs the Denzi helmet peer family. (1) OUTLINE WEIGHT: Peers show thin, crisp 1px outlines with careful anti-aliasing; candidate has noticeably chunkier, blocky outlines inconsistent with family. (2) OUTLINE COLOR: Peers use pure black; candidate mixes dark brown into outline — minor but measurable drift. (3) SHADOW DIRECTION: Peers share consistent upper-left light source with clean shadow gradient; candidate's shading feels centered/ambiguous, no clear directional read. (4) SHADOW COUNT: Peers use 1 clear shadow layer; candidate layers multiple tones without the same discipline — passable but loose. (5) HIGHLIGHT INTENSITY: Peers use restrained, single-pixel specular dots; candidate's dome has a large, highly saturated green highlight zone that blows past family norm. (6) COLOR TEMPERATURE: HARD FAIL — peers are unanimously cool-neutral metallic silver/grey with small warm accents (red plume, gold trim). Candidate is warm brown/rust body + vivid teal-green visor. Opposite temperature; near-zero palette overlap. (7) DETAIL DENSITY: Candidate packs more surface-detail complexity (rivet texture, circuit-like dome pattern) than any peer at equivalent read distance — over-detailed for family. (8) PROPORTION: Peers are vertically-biased helmet silhouettes; candidate is a wide, spherical deep-sea helmet — tolerable as different item type, but the near-circular silhouette reads as a different shape language. (9) RENDERING STYLE: Most damaging — candidate appears to be rendered at a significantly larger canvas and then displayed at peer scale, producing a 'zoomed-out chunky' pixel density vs the fine Denzi pixel-work of peers. Sprite pixel size does not match family grid. (10) PALETTE OVERLAP: Minimal. Only black outlines are shared. Warm brown, rust, bright teal/green appear nowhere in peer sheet. (11) CHARM LEVEL: Peers project cute-but-serious fantasy warrior aesthetic. Candidate reads industrial/steampunk sci-fi — mismatched genre charm. Overall: too many first-order style breaks to be a marginal; candidate needs palette realignment to cool metallics, outline weight reduction, highlight restraint, and ideally a re-render at the family pixel density.
